suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble overlaps Office/Dashboard tabs on mobile
suggestions.description: On mobile (375px), Bloberto's speech bubble covers the Office tab, making it impossible to tap. The bubble z-index is higher than the tab bar. Either move the speech bubble below the tabs, reduce its width on mobile, or add a dismiss-on-tap behavior. This is the worst mobile UX issue right now.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text is unreadable on mobile — vertical letter stacking
suggestions.description: The whiteboard on mobile shows letters stacked vertically (W/H/I/T/E/B/O/A/R/D) because the container is too narrow. The actual vibe text inside is also clipped. This has been flagged in multiple prior reviews. Either hide the whiteboard on small screens or rotate it 90° with a transform. Carry-over from reviews #35-37.

suggestions.title: Sofia's name label is RED and shouting compared to other workers
suggestions.description: In the desktop screenshot, Sofia's desk label shows 'SOFIA' in bright red/uppercase while Carlos and Maya have normal gray labels. This is because her QA role color (#f43f5e) is applied to the active worker label. It looks like an error state, not a 'working' state. The name color for active workers should be consistent — use the status color (green for working), not the role color.

suggestions.title: Mobile layout: desk grid doesn't reflow — desks overlap and clip
suggestions.description: The office uses absolute positioning (DESKS array with left/top percentages). On mobile, desks overlap each other because the percentages don't account for the narrower viewport. Dave and Luna's desks are cramped. Consider a CSS grid or flexbox layout for mobile that stacks desks vertically or in a 2-column layout.

suggestions.title: App.jsx (1366 lines) and Office.jsx (1552 lines) need splitting
suggestions.description: Both files are massive. WorkerCard, StatsBar, ActivityLog, and Dashboard are all self-contained components inside App.jsx that could be extracted. Office.jsx has CharacterAvatar, Desk components, and all the SVG definitions that could be in separate files. This is a carry-over but it's getting worse with each feature addition.

suggestions.title: All desk screen SVGs are defined inline as constants — should be lazy or memoized per vibe
suggestions.description: DESK_SCREEN_SVG object at the top of Office.jsx contains 15 SVG definitions (5 normal + 5 error + partial). These are created on every module load. They include animated elements that run continuously. Consider wrapping error-state SVGs in a lazy loader so they only mount when a worker is in error state.

suggestions.title: No loading state or skeleton when dashboard data is being fetched
suggestions.description: When switching to Dashboard tab, if data hasn't loaded yet, the user sees either nothing or a flash. App.jsx fetches from GitHub raw URLs with cache-busting. There should be a skeleton/shimmer state while waiting for the first fetch to resolve.

suggestions.title: Tab buttons use role assumptions without proper ARIA tab pattern
suggestions.description: The Office/Dashboard tabs should use role='tablist' on the container and role='tab' + aria-selected on each button. This was flagged in prior reviews as a role='radio' issue. The fix is straightforward: tablist/tab/tabpanel ARIA pattern. Carry-over.

suggestions.title: Coffee cup and plant decorations clip against right edge on tablet widths
suggestions.description: At ~768px width, the coffee cup ('COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT') and the gradient rectangle (sunrise panel?) extend past the office border. The office container has overflow:hidden but the elements are positioned with percentage-based right offsets that don't scale well at intermediate widths.

suggestions.title: Add a quick stats summary to the Office tab header
suggestions.description: The office view shows characters at desks but no quick summary of how many are working/idle/error without switching to Dashboard. A small pill badge next to the 'Office' tab showing '4 working' or similar would add context without cluttering the view.

suggestions.title: VIBE constants are scattered across 4 files — centralize in vibe.js
suggestions.description: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS, VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS, DOOR_LABELS, LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS are in Office.jsx. VIBE_WHITEBOARD is in constants.js. VIBE_QUOTES are in quotes.js. getTeamVibe is in vibe.js. All vibe-related constants should live in one place for consistency.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar fallback emoji SVG text not hidden from screen readers
suggestions.description: When CharacterAvatar renders the generic emoji fallback (Office.jsx), the SVG contains a <text> element that screen readers will announce in addition to the aria-label. Add aria-hidden='true' to the inner text element. Carry-over from #37.

suggestions.title: Light mode is half-baked — either finish it or remove the toggle
suggestions.description: The sun/moon toggle exists but light mode produces washed-out office floor and low-contrast elements. Only 6 CSS selectors handle light mode in Office.css. Either add comprehensive light theme variables or hide the toggle and commit to dark-only. Carry-over.

regression_notes: Score stable at 3.7 (vs 3.75 in #37). The mobile whiteboard and ARIA tab issues remain unfixed across 4+ reviews — these need to be prioritized or explicitly deferred. Sofia's red name label is a new visual regression likely introduced when role colors were applied to active worker labels. The desktop experience remains strong. Mobile is where the pain is.
kudos: Bloberto's vibe-reactive face animations are genuinely delightful — the 'crushing' sparkle eyes are chef's kiss
The weather metaphor for team vibe (Clear skies, Storm conditions) is creative and immediately readable
Keyboard shortcuts (1/2/R/Esc) are a nice power-user touch
The dynamic speech bubbles with vibe-specific humor give real personality
Coffee tracker and office plants add warmth without cluttering
